title: oreocnide frutescens subsp. insignis
intro: Oreocnide frutescens subsp. insignis, also known as Mountain Pepper Vine, is a climbing plant native to Australia, characterized by its woody stems and edible fruit. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of compounds such as oreocarpol and oreocarpidine. The health benefits of Oreocnide frutescens subsp. insignis include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its ability to support immune function and protect against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of a tincture or decoction, and its leaves and fruit can also be consumed fresh or dried as a spice.
